message: Trays are still cooling from yesterday. Heat's off, ratios held. We start with cornmeal ratio - too much makes it dense, too little and the structure collapses around the filling.
The structure-around-filling thing is the actual story here - that's what people need to taste, not some riff on American comfort food history.
Yeah, the filling pushes through if the cornmeal's too thin - so what's the exact ratio you locked yesterday?
One part cornmeal to two parts flour, by weight - anything else and you lose the bite. Steph, write that down before someone asks tomorrow.
Got it locked - one to two cornmeal to flour, that's the baseline we're shipping with.
Good - that ratio holds. The cornmeal's doing the structural work, so copy needs to respect that, not bury it under nostalgia. We're solid.
